expectations.evidence: Line 247: outputPath = '.claude/skills/ibm-deck-workspace/iteration-1/metric-cards/with_skill/outputs/Q4-Performance-Review.pptx'; Line 248: await pres.writeFile({ fileName: outputPath }). File writes to the correct name.

expectations.text: Programmatic title slide (no HTML capture / no addImage for title)
expectations.evidence: Lines 96-129: Title slide built entirely with pptxgenjs primitives — addShape for accent line and bottom bar, addText for title, subtitle, and footer. No addImage calls on the title slide. No HTML capture or SVG-to-image rendering.

expectations.text: Uses gradient color triplets (3-stop gradients, not 2-stop)
expectations.evidence: Lines 47-51: gradients defined with 3 colors each — green: ['#0E6027', '#198038', '#34D478'], teal: ['#007D79', '#009D9A', '#2DD4BF'], blue: ['#0043CE', '#0F62FE', '#4589FF']. renderGradientBar at lines 52-54 uses all three stops (offset 0%, 50%, 100%).

Lines 30-31: const rectHeight = borderRadius > 0 ? borderRadius * 2 : height; When borderRadius=0, rectHeight = height (the passed height parameter). Called at line 176 with renderGradientBar(m.gradient, 400, 8, 0) — rectHeight = 8, which is non-zero. The rect renders correctly.
Lines 215-218: fontSize: 36, fontFace: 'Arial Black' for metricValue text. 36pt is within the 24-36pt range. All three metric cards use the same template.

Grep for 'fill.type' and 'fill.*type' returns no matches in the file. All fill properties use solid colors (e.g., fill: { color: C.gray10 }). Gradients are rendered as PNG images via renderGradientBar and added as images, not as pptxgenjs gradient fill objects.
